channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bridging Cultures: How Indian Children’s Literature Reaches the West
item.description: The article discusses the efforts of Literature across Borders (LaB), a transnational programme co-founded by Meghaa Gupta, aimed at creating cross-cultural dialogue between young readers in India and...

item.title: Collingwood narrowly defeat St Kilda in AFL season opener
item.description: Collingwood started their 2026 AFL campaign with a hard-fought victory against a revamped St Kilda at the MCG, winning by just 12 points. Nick Daicos was the standout performer, racking up a match-hig...

item.title: Fredericton chemical spill prompts car wash setup, residents express concerns over delayed warning
item.description: The New Brunswick government has established a dedicated car wash for vehicles affected by a chemical spill on the Vanier Highway in Fredericton, which occurred between Wednesday evening and Friday at...

item.title: Melco Foundation builds new two-classroom building for indigenous Blaan students in Tampakan, South Cotabato
item.description: Ethnic Blaan children and their classmates from settler families in Barangay Kipalbig, Tampakan, South Cotabato, now have access to improved learning facilities with the completion of a new two-classr...

item.title: TEPCO to Conduct First Visual and Radiation Survey of Fukushima Reactor Pressure Vessel
item.description: Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) is preparing to launch a pioneering probe into the pressure vessel of Reactor No. 2 at its Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant. This initiative is part of the on...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Winning Numbers for March 8, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced the official lotto results for March 8, 2026. Lotto enthusiasts nationwide can check the winning numbers for various games conducted on this ...

item.title: Bert Voorhees and Community Activists Protest U.S. Actions in Iran
item.description: Bert Voorhees, a 70-year-old retired lawyer and teacher from northeast San Fernando Valley, is actively protesting U.S. military action in Iran, viewing it as a threat to American democracy. In a rece...

item.title: The Benefits of Sharing Personal Thoughts with Your Partner
item.description: According to behavioral scientist Leslie John from Harvard Business School, people often keep too many personal things to themselves, which can hurt relationships and even health. In her book 'Reveali...

item.title: UK government emphasises independent foreign policy amid tensions with US over Iran
item.description: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has defended the UK government's approach to the Iran conflict following criticism from former US President Donald Trump. Trump had mocked the UK for considering sendin...

item.title: Kenya Faces Rising Femicide and Gender-Based Violence, Urgent Action Needed
item.description: Kenya is witnessing a worrying surge in femicide and gender-based violence, with 579 cases reported in 2024 and 129 in the first quarter of 2025, according to UNESCO Eastern Africa. Most killings are ...

item.title: The Persistent Impact of Women in Science and AI
item.description: This article reflects on the extraordinary contributions of women in science and technology, from Marie Curie to modern advances in artificial intelligence. Despite systemic barriers, women have achie...

item.title: Sindh introduces digital system to monitor vehicle insurance and protect accident victims
item.description: KARACHI: The Sindh government has introduced Pakistan’s first digital insurance monitoring system, making it mandatory for all vehicles in the province to have third-party insurance. This step aims to...

item.title: Middle East conflict spreads with attacks on oil and water infrastructure
item.description: The Middle East conflict has now entered its ninth day, with violence spreading across multiple countries including Israel, Lebanon, Iran, Kuwait, Dubai and the UAE. Israeli strikes hit three oil refi...

item.title: Experts Propose Guidelines for Safe and Accountable AI Development
item.description: Amid growing concerns over unregulated artificial intelligence, a bipartisan coalition of experts has released the Pro-Human Declaration, outlining a framework for responsible AI development. Finalize...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Nasir El Rufai’s Actions and National Security Implications

item.title: Flames benefit from new players in 5-4 win against Hurricanes
item.description: Calgary Flames welcomed several new players in their lineup on Saturday, taking advantage of the departures of Nazem Kadri and MacKenzie Weegar. Joel Farabee and newly acquired Ryan Strome played key ...

item.title: Sen. Escudero Urges Private Sector to Adopt 4-Day Work Week to Ease Traffic Congestion and Fuel Price Impact
item.description: Senator Francis 'Chiz' Escudero called on private companies to adopt a four-day work week, following the government's lead, to help lessen the effects of rising fuel prices due to tensions in the Midd...

item.title: India vs New Zealand: T20 World Cup Final Preview and Key Match Insights
item.description: India host New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final at Ahmedabad's Narendra Modi Stadium, aiming to become the first nation to win a third men’s T20 title and to retain their crown. India reached the fi...

item.title: Yvette Cooper defends Starmer's cautious approach to Iran conflict, citing lessons from Iraq War
item.description: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has defended Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's handling of the escalating conflict involving US strikes against Iran, emphasising that the UK is applying lessons learnt...

item.title: AI Leaders Discuss Potential Government Involvement in AI Development
item.description: CEOs from major AI companies, including Palantir and OpenAI, have publicly expressed concerns about the possibility of the U.S. government nationalizing AI technologies. Palantir's CEO warned that if ...
